title: Bitfarms Reports Third Quarter 2025 Results
text: Bitfarms Q3 2025 results show total revenue of $69 million from continuing operations representing 156% year-over-year growth from $27 million in Q3 2024. Adjusted EBITDA was $20 million representing 28% margin, up significantly from $2 million (8% margin) in prior year quarter. Gross mining margin declined to 35% from 44%, reflecting increased operating costs. CEO Ben Gagnon stated pivot from international Bitcoin miner to North American energy and digital infrastructure company. Key infrastructure projects include Washington site converting to support advanced liquid cooling and GPU workloads targeting completion by December 2026. Panther Creek in Pennsylvania expanding from 60 MW to 410 MW of firm power capacity. Sharon, Pennsylvania site recently acquired converting 30 MW of existing capacity with 80 MW additional substation planned for late 2026. Company closed $588 million convertible notes offering at 1.375% interest. Total liquidity reached $814 million in cash plus unencumbered Bitcoin holdings. Mining operations remain efficient at 18 W/TH earning 520 BTC in Q3. Argentina and Paraguay operations discontinued generating $14 million revenue but $35 million net loss in Q3 2025 due to asset impairment charges.
